section_title: Mode of dealing with order to be enforced by other Courts.-- Where any order made by one Court is to be
content:
284. Mode of dealing with order to be enforced by other Courts.-- Where any order made by one Court is to be
enforced by another Court, a certified copy of the order so made shall be produced to the proper officer of the Court required
to enforce the same, and the production of such certified copy shall be sufficient evidence of such order having been made;
and thereupon the last mentioned Court shall take the requisite steps in the matter for enforcing the order, in the same
manner as if it were the order of the Court enforcing the same.
